info.description: This problem set focuses on fungal pathogens and antifungal agents. The difficulty may vary a lot through out the set, since this set contains a mix of four different cognitive levels of complexity (recall, understand, apply, analyze). The questions are already reviewed, so mistakes should have already been minimized, but if you come across one, you can report through the Forms in the "Info and Links".

q1.prompt: A 30-year-old patient with thalassemia major presents with progressive leg pain and discoloration. Imaging reveals extensive arteritis, and a biopsy of the affected vessel demonstrates wide, non-septate hyphae. The causative organism is identified as belonging to the Kingdom Chromista rather than Fungi. Which of the following molecular features is characteristic of this pathogen?

options.label: Cell wall composed primarily of chitin
Presence of a thick mucicarmine-staining capsule

q2.prompt: During the microscopic examination of a skin scraping from a patient with a dermatophyte infection, the laboratory technician identifies specific morphological structures. Which of the following terms describes barrel-shaped cells that result from the fragmentation of hyphal filaments?

q3.prompt: A clinical microbiologist observing *Candida albicans* notes its ability to spontaneously and reversibly change both its colony morphology and microscopic appearance to adapt to different host environments. Which of the following terms best describes this specific virulence mechanism?

q4.prompt: A 42-year-old male with a history of hematologic malignancy is diagnosed with invasive aspergillosis and initiated on antifungal therapy. Shortly after starting the medication, he reports transient visual disturbances, specifically describing seeing "wavy lines" and flashing lights. Which of the following medications was most likely administered?

q5.prompt: A 55-year-old diabetic patient presents with rhinocerebral mucormycosis. The clinical team considers initiating therapy with a newer triazole that is administered as the prodrug isavuconazonium sulfate. Which of the following is a contraindication for the use of this specific medication?

q6.prompt: A 39-year-old male with HIV presents with headache and lethargy. The medical team suspects cryptococcal meningitis and performs a rapid diagnostic test on the cerebrospinal fluid that utilizes antibody-coated latex particles to detect specific fungal components. Which of the following is the specific target of this diagnostic test?

q7.prompt: A 29-year-old male with a history of HIV (CD4 count 45 cells/mm³) presents to a clinic in Northern Thailand with a two-week history of fever, a nonproductive cough, and significant weight loss. Physical examination reveals generalized lymphadenopathy, hepatosplenomegaly, and numerous skin lesions consisting of small, centrally umbilicated papules on the face and trunk. A skin biopsy is performed and sent for histopathological evaluation and fungal culture. Histopathological examination of the skin biopsy specimen is likely to reveal which of the following microscopic morphologies?

q8.prompt: A 29-year-old male with a history of HIV (CD4 count 45 cells/mm³) presents to a clinic in Northern Thailand with a two-week history of fever, a nonproductive cough, and significant weight loss. Physical examination reveals generalized lymphadenopathy, hepatosplenomegaly, and numerous skin lesions consisting of small, centrally umbilicated papules on the face and trunk. Which of the following is the primary mode of transmission for this fungal infection?

q9.prompt: A 52-year-old female with poorly controlled Type 2 Diabetes Mellitus presents with adherent white, curd-like plaques on her tongue and inner cheeks. She also reports a thick, white, "cottage cheese" vaginal discharge and intense pruritus. A scraping of the oral lesions is incubated in serum at 37°C for three hours, revealing the formation of true hyphal extensions without constriction at the point of origin. Which of the following vaginal pH and microscopic findings would be most consistent with the patient's genital symptoms?

options.label: pH > 4.5; epithelial cells coated with bacteria (clue cells)

q10.prompt: A 35-year-old male with a known history of HIV and a CD4 count of 40 cells/mm³ is brought to the emergency department with a three-week history of progressive headache, intermittent fever, and increasing confusion. On physical examination, the patient is lethargic and demonstrates mild nuchal rigidity. India ink preparation of the cerebrospinal fluid (CSF) reveals numerous round budding yeasts surrounded by a distinct, large clear halo. While receiving the initial induction therapy, the patient must be closely monitored for adverse effects. Which of the following clinical findings is specifically associated with the oral agent utilized in the induction regimen for this disease?

options.label: Abnormal vision, including wavy lines or flashing lights
Negative inotropic effect and congestive heart failure
Orange-tan discoloration of tears and saliva
